title: FDA Approves First Oral PCSK9 Inhibitor to Lower LDL Cholesterol in Adults with High Cholesterol
excerpt: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration approved Lipfendra (enlicitide), the first oral inhibitor of proprotein convertase subtilisin/kexin type 9 (PCSK9), as an adjunct to diet and exercise to reduce low-density lipoprotein cholesterol (LDL-C), or “bad” cholesterol, in adults with hypercholesterolemi
content_html:
Recall Alert

An update on the Cyclospora outbreak available here.

FDA News Release

First oral PCSK9 inhibitor provides an additional treatment option for adults with hypercholesterolemia

For Immediate Release:
July 17, 2026

The U.S. Food and Drug Administration approved Lipfendra (enlicitide), the first oral inhibitor of proprotein convertase subtilisin/kexin type 9 (PCSK9), as an adjunct to diet and exercise to reduce low-density lipoprotein cholesterol (LDL-C), or “bad” cholesterol, in adults with hypercholesterolemia, including those with heterozygous familial hypercholesterolemia (HeFH).

PCSK9 inhibitors have previously been available only as injectable therapies. Lipfendra is administered as a once-daily oral tablet and provides an additional treatment option for adults who require further LDL-C reduction despite existing therapies. The approval also reflects continued advances in the development of therapies for chronic cardiovascular disease management.

“Cardiovascular disease remains the leading cause of death in the United States, and elevated LDL cholesterol is one of its most important modifiable risk factors,” said Michael Davis, M.D., Ph.D., Acting Director of the FDA’s Center for Drug Evaluation and Research. “This approval provides an additional treatment option for adults with hypercholesterolemia and reflects the FDA’s broader commitment to supporting meaningful advances in patient access and tackling chronic diseases.”

Hypercholesterolemia occurs when there is too much LDL-C in the blood. Over time, excess LDL-C can accumulate within the walls of arteries, contributing to the formation of plaques that narrow blood vessels and restrict blood flow. If a plaque ruptures, it can trigger the formation of a blood clot, increasing the risk of heart attack or stroke. Because high cholesterol typically causes no symptoms, many people are unaware they have the condition until it is detected through routine blood testing. Diet, physical inactivity, excess body weight, and inherited genetic conditions can all contribute to elevated cholesterol levels.

Several classes of medications are available to lower LDL-C, including statins, ezetimibe, and injectable PCSK9 inhibitors. Lipfendra is the first oral therapy to inhibit PCSK9.

The efficacy and safety of Lipfendra were evaluated in two randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled clinical trials involving 3,207 adults with hypercholesterolemia, including patients with and without HeFH, who were receiving maximally tolerated statin therapy. The primary endpoint in both studies was the percent change in LDL-C from baseline to Week 24 compared with placebo.

In the first trial, which enrolled adults with established atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease (ASCVD) or those at high risk for ASCVD, participants had a mean baseline LDL-C of 96 mg/dL. Treatment with Lipfendra resulted in an average 56% reduction in LDL-C at Week 24 compared with placebo.

In the second trial, which enrolled adults with HeFH, participants had a mean baseline LDL-C of 119 mg/dL. Treatment with Lipfendra resulted in an average 59% reduction in LDL-C at Week 24 compared with placebo.

In the first trial, the frequency of adverse reactions was similar between patients treated with Lipfendra and those receiving placebo. In the second trial, the most common adverse reactions occurring more frequently with Lipfendra than placebo were diarrhea and dizziness. Across both trials, discontinuation rates due to adverse reactions were comparable between the Lipfendra-treated and placebo-recipient groups.

Lipfendra received Priority Review for this indication. The application was also reviewed under the Commissioner’s National Priority Voucher (CNPV) pilot program, which is intended to help accelerate the review of therapies that address national public health priorities.

The approval was granted to Merck Sharp & Dohme LLC.

Consumer:
888-INFO-FDA

The primary endpoint in both studies was the percent change in LDL-C from baseline to Week 24 compared with placebo.

sentences.en: The efficacy and safety of Lipfendra were evaluated in two randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled clinical trials involving 3,207 adults with hypercholesterolemia, including patients with and without HeFH, who were receiving maximally tolerated statin therapy.
sentences.zh: Lipfendra 的疗效和安全性在两项随机、双盲、安慰剂对照临床试验中得到评估，试验涉及 3,207 名高胆固醇血症成人，包括患有和不患有 HeFH 的患者，他们正在接受最大耐受的他汀类药物治疗。

sentences.en: The primary endpoint in both studies was the percent change in LDL-C from baseline to Week 24 compared with placebo.
sentences.zh: 这两项研究的主要终点是与安慰剂相比，LDL-C 从基线到第 24 周的变化百分比。

blocks.html_en: In the first trial, which enrolled adults with established atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease (ASCVD) or those at high risk for ASCVD, participants had a mean baseline LDL-C of 96 mg/dL. Treatment with Lipfendra resulted in an average 56% reduction in LDL-C at Week 24 compared with placebo.

sentences.en: In the first trial, which enrolled adults with established atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease (ASCVD) or those at high risk for ASCVD, participants had a mean baseline LDL-C of 96 mg/dL.
sentences.zh: 第一项试验招募了患有动脉粥样硬化性心血管疾病 (ASCVD) 或 ASCVD 高风险的成年人，参与者的平均基线 LDL-C 为 96 mg/dL。

sentences.en: Treatment with Lipfendra resulted in an average 56% reduction in LDL-C at Week 24 compared with placebo.
sentences.zh: 与安慰剂相比，使用 Lipfendra 治疗第 24 周时 LDL-C 平均降低 56%。

blocks.html_en: In the second trial, which enrolled adults with HeFH, participants had a mean baseline LDL-C of 119 mg/dL. Treatment with Lipfendra resulted in an average 59% reduction in LDL-C at Week 24 compared with placebo.

sentences.en: In the second trial, which enrolled adults with HeFH, participants had a mean baseline LDL-C of 119 mg/dL.
sentences.zh: 在第二项试验中，招募了患有 HeFH 的成人，参与者的平均基线 LDL-C 为 119 mg/dL。

sentences.en: Treatment with Lipfendra resulted in an average 59% reduction in LDL-C at Week 24 compared with placebo.
sentences.zh: 与安慰剂相比，使用 Lipfendra 治疗第 24 周时 LDL-C 平均降低 59%。

blocks.html_en: In the first trial, the frequency of adverse reactions was similar between patients treated with Lipfendra and those receiving placebo. In the second trial, the most common adverse reactions occurring more frequently with Lipfendra than placebo were diarrhea and dizziness. Across both trials, discontinuation rates due to adverse reactions were comparable between the Lipfendra-treated and placebo-recipient groups.

sentences.en: In the first trial, the frequency of adverse reactions was similar between patients treated with Lipfendra and those receiving placebo.
sentences.zh: 在第一项试验中，利芬德拉治疗患者和安慰剂治疗患者的不良反应频率相似。

sentences.en: In the second trial, the most common adverse reactions occurring more frequently with Lipfendra than placebo were diarrhea and dizziness.
sentences.zh: 在第二项试验中，与安慰剂相比，利芬德拉最常见的不良反应是腹泻和头晕。

sentences.en: Across both trials, discontinuation rates due to adverse reactions were comparable between the Lipfendra-treated and placebo-recipient groups.
sentences.zh: 在这两项试验中，利芬德拉治疗组和安慰剂治疗组因不良反应而停药的比率相当。

blocks.html_en: Lipfendra received Priority Review for this indication. The application was also reviewed under the Commissioner’s National Priority Voucher (CNPV) pilot program, which is intended to help accelerate the review of therapies that address national public health priorities.

sentences.en: Lipfendra received Priority Review for this indication.
sentences.zh: Lipfendra 已获得该适应症的优先审查。

sentences.en: The application was also reviewed under the Commissioner’s National Priority Voucher (CNPV) pilot program, which is intended to help accelerate the review of therapies that address national public health priorities.
sentences.zh: 该申请还根据专员的国家优先券（CNPV）试点计划进行了审查，该计划旨在帮助加快对解决国家公共卫生优先事项的疗法的审查。

blocks.html_en: The approval was granted to Merck Sharp & Dohme LLC.

sentences.en: The approval was granted to Merck Sharp & Dohme LLC.
sentences.zh: 该批准已授予 Merck Sharp & Dohme LLC。

content_html_zh:
召回警报

有关环孢子虫爆发的最新信息，请点击此处。

FDA 新闻稿

首个口服 PCSK9 抑制剂为成人高胆固醇血症提供了额外的治疗选择

立即发布：
July 17, 2026

美国食品和药物管理局批准 Lipfendra (enlicitide) 是首个口服前蛋白转化酶枯草杆菌蛋白酶/kexin 9 型 (PCSK9) 抑制剂，作为饮食和运动的辅助药物，用于降低成人高胆固醇血症，包括杂合子家族性高胆固醇血症 (HeFH) 患者的低密度脂蛋白胆固醇 (LDL-C) 或“坏”胆固醇。

PCSK9 抑制剂此前仅作为注射疗法提供。 Lipfendra 以每日一次口服片剂的形式给药，为尽管现有疗法仍需要进一步降低 LDL-C 的成年人提供了额外的治疗选择。 此次批准还反映出慢性心血管疾病管理疗法开发的持续进步。

FDA 药物评价与研究中心代理主任、医学博士、哲学博士迈克尔·戴维斯 (Michael Davis) 表示：“心血管疾病仍然是美国的首要死因，而低密度脂蛋白胆固醇升高是其最重要的可改变危险因素之一。” “这项批准为患有高胆固醇血症的成人提供了额外的治疗选择，并反映了 FDA 对支持患者获取和应对慢性疾病方面的有意义进展的更广泛承诺。”

当血液中 LDL-C 过多时，就会发生高胆固醇血症。 随着时间的推移，过量的低密度脂蛋白胆固醇会积聚在动脉壁内，导致斑块的形成，从而缩小血管并限制血流。 如果斑块破裂，就会引发血栓的形成，增加心脏病发作或中风的风险。 由于高胆固醇通常不会引起任何症状，因此许多人在通过常规血液检测发现之前并不知道自己患有这种疾病。 饮食、缺乏身体活动、体重过重和遗传性疾病都可能导致胆固醇水平升高。

有几类药物可用于降低 LDL-C，包括他汀类药物、依折麦布和注射用 PCSK9 抑制剂。 Lipfendra 是第一个抑制 PCSK9 的口服疗法。

Lipfendra 的疗效和安全性在两项随机、双盲、安慰剂对照临床试验中得到评估，试验涉及 3,207 名高胆固醇血症成人，包括患有和不患有 HeFH 的患者，他们正在接受最大耐受的他汀类药物治疗。 这两项研究的主要终点是与安慰剂相比，LDL-C 从基线到第 24 周的变化百分比。

第一项试验招募了患有动脉粥样硬化性心血管疾病 (ASCVD) 或 ASCVD 高风险的成年人，参与者的平均基线 LDL-C 为 96 mg/dL。 与安慰剂相比，使用 Lipfendra 治疗第 24 周时 LDL-C 平均降低 56%。

在第二项试验中，招募了患有 HeFH 的成人，参与者的平均基线 LDL-C 为 119 mg/dL。 与安慰剂相比，使用 Lipfendra 治疗第 24 周时 LDL-C 平均降低 59%。

在第一项试验中，利芬德拉治疗患者和安慰剂治疗患者的不良反应频率相似。 在第二项试验中，与安慰剂相比，利芬德拉最常见的不良反应是腹泻和头晕。 在这两项试验中，利芬德拉治疗组和安慰剂治疗组因不良反应而停药的比率相当。

Lipfendra 已获得该适应症的优先审查。 该申请还根据专员的国家优先券（CNPV）试点计划进行了审查，该计划旨在帮助加快对解决国家公共卫生优先事项的疗法的审查。

该批准已授予 Merck Sharp & Dohme LLC。

消费者：888-INFO-FDA
